name: Beale AFB
tagline: The Air Force's high-altitude reconnaissance hub, flying the U-2 and Global Hawk in California's Sacramento Valley.
_note: Air Force reconnaissance/ISR base (Yuba County, Sacramento Valley CA). CONUS full template (BAH MHA CA033 Beale AFB; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). 9th Reconnaissance Wing flies the U-2 Dragon Lady and RQ-4 Global Hawk. Medical: 9th Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (beale.af.mil, 9 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). California is TRICARE West. More affordable than coastal CA. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 95901 (Marysville).

data.Location: Yuba County, CA (Sacramento Valley, near Marysville/Yuba City)
data.Aircraft: U-2 Dragon Lady and RQ-4 Global Hawk
data.Setting: Inland northern California (more affordable than the coast)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Beale Air Force Base is a key Air Force reconnaissance hub, home to the 9th Reconnaissance Wing, which flies the high-altitude U-2 Dragon Lady and the RQ-4 Global Hawk to collect intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance.
In California's Sacramento Valley near Marysville and Yuba City, it offers a more affordable inland-California lifestyle, with Sacramento, Lake Tahoe, and the Sierra foothills all within reach.

data.subtitle: Beale is in the more affordable Sacramento Valley, not pricey coastal California. The main choice is which valley town fits your commute and schools, with on-base housing also available.
intro: Compared with coastal California, the Yuba-Sutter area is much more affordable, so BAH stretches further. On-base housing is available; many also live off base in the nearby towns.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Marysville, Yuba City, Wheatland, and toward the Sacramento suburbs.

recommendations.advice: Wheatland and Marysville sit near the base for a short commute.
Yuba City and the Yuba-Sutter area offer more shopping, dining, and schools nearby.
Lincoln, Roseville, and the north Sacramento suburbs have more options and good schools, a longer commute south.
On-base housing is available and convenient; weigh it against the affordable local market.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Beale AFB site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Beale AFB has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA CA033 (Beale AFB)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Wheatland, Marysville Joint Unified, and Yuba City area districts serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via the Yuba-Sutter and Sacramento-area network

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the affordable-for-California Yuba-Sutter market makes buying a real option for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the valley before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The relatively affordable valley market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a town and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but the Yuba-Sutter area is affordable by California standards, so buying is worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on the Yuba-Sutter area before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and California vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Wheatland, Marysville, or Yuba City area) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the 9th Medical Group clinic or network providers.

data.a: It's the Air Force's high-altitude reconnaissance hub, home to the 9th Reconnaissance Wing flying the U-2 Dragon Lady and the RQ-4 Global Hawk.

data.q: Is it expensive like the rest of California?
data.a: No. The Yuba-Sutter area in the Sacramento Valley is much more affordable than coastal California, so BAH stretches further.
On-base housing is available; many live off base. Wheatland and Marysville are closest; Yuba City has more amenities; the north Sacramento suburbs are an option farther south.
Beale is MHA CA033. Use the calculator above for your grade.
